review:
The Independent comprises a great laid-spot for beers for the afternoon and evening, especially on a late Sunday morning and afternoon if you want some chill vibes. 

The latter point is what drew us to The Independent, where I could knock back a couple of beers, including noshing on an egg & cheese croissant sandwich with bacon that was quite satisfying and tantalizing on the senses. 

Originally a gas station per the structural exterior, The Independent works very well as a Biergarten, with its cedar wood bar and walls on the inside and chill outdoor seating.

They have a pervasive selection of craft beer on tap and in a bottle and can. Many of the beers come from Tampa-St. Pete breweries, but others come from all over the country and the globe. 

The highlight beer I had was the Oude Geuze Boon by Brouwerij Boon that was a delicious lambic that definitely had lovely citrus and sour palate. Between the beer and the breakfast, I was pleased with my experience at The Independent. And the beertenders were chill and affable, which was a plus as we hung out at the bar on a Sunday late morning.
